samples.text: A number is both even and odd at the same time. Is this logically consistent?
A single number is simultaneously even and odd. Does this make logical sense?
One integer is both even and odd at once. Is this logically consistent?
Even and odd are mutually exclusive for an integer. A number cannot be both. Is the described situation logically consistent?
A number is both even and odd at the same time. This topic was discussed last week. Is this logically consistent?
